llm_verdict.message:
{
  "plan_description": "Remediation Plan:
- Objective: Ensure /etc/shadow has restrictive permissions as required by the STIG rule file_permissions_etc_shadow.
- Action: Execute a single command to set the file mode to 0000, removing all read, write, and execute bits for user, group, and others. This aligns with the recommendation "chmod u-xwrs,g-xwrs,o-xwrt /etc/shadow" which effectively results in 0000 permissions. The command will be:
  chmod 0000 /etc/shadow
- Rationale: Setting /etc/shadow to 0000 ensures no non-privileged access to the password hash file, satisfying the security rule without affecting system functionality because root bypasses file permissions for reading the file.
- No configuration files will be edited; only a permission change command is required.
- Verification will be performed by the automatic scan after execution."
}
